skill.name: domain-driven-design
skill.description: Plan and route Domain-Driven Design work from strategic modeling to tactical implementation and evented architecture patterns.
skill.summary: Guide Domain-Driven Design planning, boundaries, artifacts, and routing decisions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and ordinary DDD wording as weak cryptography. Manual review found documentation-only guidance with no executable code, network access, secret access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Skill References Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 42-51 list skill routing targets and a reference file in Markdown inline code. Lines 64-67 show a plain text example. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or command invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is Markdown documentation and example text, not Ruby or shell code. There is no executable file or runtime path that could evaluate these backticks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern Matched Design Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain the skill name, description, heading text, or DDD guidance. No cryptographic API, weak hash, cipher mode, or security-sensitive implementation appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed content contains architecture guidance only. The weak-crypto alerts appear to come from word fragments such as design, not from cryptographic code.

content.value_statement: Complex product domains often suffer from unclear boundaries, unstable models, and team-level vocabulary conflicts. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through practical DDD planning, artifacts, and routing decisions.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether full Domain-Driven Design is justified for a project.
Identifies strategic DDD artifacts such as subdomains, bounded contexts, and language glossaries.
Routes tasks to related skills for strategic design, context mapping, tactical patterns, CQRS, and event sourcing.
Defines required output sections for scope, assumptions, stage, artifacts, risks, and next steps.
Provides a deliverables checklist for strategic, tactical, and evented architecture work.
limitations: Does not replace workshops with domain experts or product owners.
Does not generate framework-specific implementation code.
Should not be used to justify DDD for simple CRUD systems.
Depends on accurate business context from the user.

use_cases.title: Assess DDD Fit
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether a complex product area needs full DDD or a lighter architecture approach.

use_cases.title: Map Bounded Contexts
use_cases.description: Turn team, workflow, and integration details into candidate bounded contexts and ownership boundaries.

use_cases.title: Plan Evented Architecture
use_cases.description: Decide when CQRS, event sourcing, sagas, or projections are justified by domain needs.

prompt_templates.title: Check DDD Viability
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess whether my product area should use full Domain-Driven Design. Summarize the domain complexity, team conflicts, integration risk, and audit needs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Strategic Artifacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me draft a subdomain map, bounded context map, and glossary for this domain. Include assumptions and open questions for domain experts.

prompt_templates.title: Route Architecture Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this DDD problem and route it to the right next architecture task. Explain whether it is strategic, tactical, or evented work.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Evented Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate whether this workflow needs CQRS, event sourcing, sagas, or projections. List the domain evidence, required artifacts, and risks.

output_examples.input: A billing platform has disputed invoices, changing rules, and several teams editing the same model.
output_examples.output: The skill would classify this as a strong DDD candidate, identify billing as a core domain, and recommend bounded context mapping first.

output_examples.input: A small admin page only stores lookup values with stable rules.
output_examples.output: The skill would recommend avoiding full DDD and using a simpler CRUD approach with clear naming and lightweight validation.

output_examples.input: A fulfillment workflow spans payment, warehouse allocation, shipping, and cancellation compensation.
output_examples.output: The skill would treat this as evented architecture planning and suggest evaluating sagas, domain events, and projection rebuild needs.

best_practices: Start with the viability check before creating DDD artifacts.
Capture assumptions and open questions for domain experts.
Use event sourcing or CQRS only when domain evidence supports the added complexity.
anti_patterns: Applying full DDD to simple CRUD workflows.
Creating bounded contexts without domain expert input.
Choosing evented patterns before defining invariants and ownership boundaries.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan Domain-Driven Design work, choose artifacts, and route architecture tasks by stage.

faq.question: Can it generate application code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on architecture planning and does not provide framework-specific code generation.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it for simple CRUD systems, localized bug fixes, or domains without available business knowledge.

faq.question: Does it support event sourcing decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help decide whether event sourcing, CQRS, sagas, or projections fit the domain.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Provide business rules, team boundaries, integration pain points, audit requirements, and known domain terms.

faq.question: Is this safe for marketplace use?
faq.answer: Manual review found documentation-only content with no executable code, network behavior, or prompt injection attempts.
